Cada minuto.

meaning: every day at 9 a.m. and 5 p.m.

meaning: every hour from 9 a.m. through 5 p.m.

slate.text: cada hora de las 09:00 a las 17:00

B.note: Natural, though the comma is slightly redundant for a mid-sentence fragment.

C.note: Natural and concise; mirrors the English structure well.

D.note: 'Entre' suggests a range but is slightly less precise for a recurring hourly event than 'de... a...'

meaning: every minute from 0 through 29 past the hour

B.note: Redundant use of 'minuto' twice.

D.note: Identical to A; perfectly natural.

meaning: every 15 seconds, at 30 minutes past the hour from 9 a.m. through 5:30 p.m. on Monday through Friday

slate.text: Cada 15 segundos, al minuto 30 de cada hora, de lunes a viernes, entre las 9:00 y las 17:30.

meaning: every minute from 0 through 30 past the hour, at 9 a.m., 5 p.m., 6 p.m., and 7 p.m.

slate.text: Cada minuto del 0 al 30 a las 9:00, 17:00, 18:00 y 19:00.

A.note: Clear and accurate. Using 24h format is standard for schedules.

B.note: Essentially the same as A; slightly more formal with leading zeros.

D.note: Incorrect range; '17:00 a 19:59' implies every minute in that block, not just the 0-30 mark.

B.note: Grammatically incorrect ('a las de') and misleading ('Cada hora' implies every single hour).
